reviews.review_text: Unforgiving, but satisfying once you understand what to do

The first Frostpunk is still one of the best survival strategy games out there. The atmosphere is incredible, and every decision feels like it actually matters. You’re not just building a city, you’re constantly trying to keep people alive while making choices that can feel pretty rough. The cold, the music, the laws, and the pressure all come together really well.

Maybe it's just me, but I find this game to be infuriating. I restarted at least 3 times and failed soon after i built the beacon every time. If I say yes to a request, no matter how much I try I can't fulfill it, "discontent rises". If I say no, "discontent rises". Almost nothing will raise hope fast enough, and by the time I did raise hope, I was banished from the city to die in the cold and uninstalled.
It's a pretty good game. The only downside is that you can't use rebels as fuel for the generator.

child labour 
public execution
hardest game ever played

Compare to 11 bit's previous title - This War of Mine , it's like heaven and earth.
I find it very boring and tedious. Things are slow and not engaging. No voiceover and I certainly do not feel the tension from a survival game.
Therefore, I am going to give a downvote for FrontPunk.
Awesome game! as a new comer to city building genre, I really enjoy this game. though it is difficult it can be forgiving

Man do I love this game!! 

Even wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot cup of tea at my side, Frostpunk never fails to send a chill down my spine. Its bleak atmosphere is so immersive that I often find myself shivering alongside my people every time the generator shuts down right as the temperature drops 2 levels... 

Ah, well, and the gameplay is simply amazing :) Replaying the same scenarios never gets boring.
Endorse yourself in the cold North, try to keep civilization going without losing your humanity. Good Luck. Lovely game, one of a kind!
u can enjoy this game for one playthrough, after u finish the main story while fail many times, the games stop feeling fun anymore, but worth it for the price

This is one of the few core games I keep returning too.  Every few months I come back to this and it still sucks me in.  The visuals, even somewhat dated are still stunning, the music is perfect, and trying out different permutations of awful decisions is addictive.  thumbs up :-)
Best single player strategy game I ever played. I can't recommend it enough, just got it on steam to play again, you can't get bored playing.

I really wanted to like this game but I find it pretty tiring for a single reason: you have to build your village and if you messes at some point, you will pay it later and will lose. One run takes several hours, but every run  feels pretty similar: the story is the same, from what I recall, events are the same. It feels like a roguelike, or more precisely like a Civilization game without the random aspect.

Yeobo I removed some games that i already own from your wishlist so you dont accidentally buy again. When you come back we should play a game together, what do you think? I think we should play a game like enshrouded together I think it would be so much fun. I love you so much happy early anniversary
A very good strategy game with a lot of replay potential. Both the main Game and the DLCs are worth it. Would 100% recomend playing and buying especially with a discount.

Too hard, on a level it's not enjoyable. Even if you follow the instructions, you can't keep up with the demands, be it coal, or food, or health. It takes a while to research steamers to heat around areas where you have to gather resources or to heat up buildings that are out of the first row from the main generator. 

I like the idea, but it gets 1 star out of 5 for being too punishing.

After brief played time of 47 hours I could say that the game could be pretty challenging. It's interesting and engaging. There's a lot of campaign missions and some challenging scenarios for hardcore players. I strongly recommend it.

yeah its fun and they did the ending recap when you win pretty satisfying. But its pretty much one big bad go beat and thats the cold. theres other senarios and issues to resolve that are sprinkled in here and there to keeo it interesting and to solve with you managment skills. But you can only manage for the extreme cold in so many ways and until a certain point you cant do anyting more. So very clever and creative game, not much interest for me in replay or extra scenarios beyond 30 hours or so.
great game, your choices makes a lot of difference in the playthrough. though the cutscenes and sound lags sometime for me... still the game runs smooth for me at 1080p 144hz

Would not recommend for full price.
Played the game on the easiest settings possible about 5 times now and it is impossible for me to enjoy. I cannot get past certain points and having to restart the entire game each time is just annoying. I got this at a discounted price of £2.49 and that is all I can say it is worth. If you do not tweak the settings to at least make the easiest of easy, playable, then I will no longer play this game. I understand a bit of a challenge but this is silly. To put it simply it is too difficult! I even googled tips and to even try to survive is so unbelievably complicated.. I simply give up. Just no,no,no,noooo

I didn’t like this game.  
The gameplay felt too complicated for me, and the tutorial didn’t explain the mechanics clearly, so I couldn’t understand what I was supposed to do.

The atmosphere is darker than I prefer. The graphics are good, but overall I didn’t enjoy the gameplay.

The game also started with a slight delay on my PC, but that wasn’t the main issue. I refunded it simply because I didn’t like the overall experience.

I don’t recommend buying it. If you enjoy games like this, you might like it more than I did, but personally it wasn’t for me.
If you’re going to buy it, I recommend getting it only when it’s on a big sale.

I remember as a kid playing Age of Empires and having so much fun. Which is why I downloaded this game. To have some fun. This is a fun game, if you like punishing and challenging games. I'd get so annoyed when I'd fail a scenario after investing many days in game. But when I was able to finish it, it was exhilirating.
This is a complex city builder game. Actions have consequences. Decisions might help your progress. They might also come to bite you in the rear. So many things to build. So many technologies to advance. So many people to order around, feed, and keep warm. If you go at it without thinking, you'll definitely lose in the long run. You have limited resources and time bound goalposts to achieve. You might not be able to even finish the campaign. You'll either have to start afresh, or if you've been saving at certain checkpoints, you'll reload. You might lose several days of progress in the process. Like a midget at a urinal, you have to be on your toes.
I love the setting and atmosphere. The city and snow is beautiful to look at. The whole world is covered in ice and it's only gonna get colder. The music gives you the sensation of being in a harsh and desolate environment, where you have to fend for yourself and others as the leader. You need to make some tough decisions to keep your settlement alive. People might agree with you. Your opinion could be unpopular, creating dissent among the settlers. If the motivation lowers or the dissent increases, beyond a certain extent, you'll be kicked out or fired. The game does give you the option to get back on track, but it's a race against time. It's punishing and thrilling at the same time.
I've been playing this for a week and I was so engrossed, I'd forget to eat cause I was worried my settlers in game were starving. When I'd quit the game after completing a scenario, 10 or 12 had passed. When you finish a scenario, you feel a sense of accomplioshment. But then the game shows you how many sacrifices you made, the compromises, the morally ambiguous choices, the lives lost. The game says, well done, but it all came at a price. And I love that.
I highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys strategy games with a lot of detail, complexity and challenge.
9.5/10 (Great game)
Just discovered it in 2026. Love it, fun, was easy even for a middle age man to pick up. Wasted alot of my time unfortunately.
Very immersive, blending survival with strategy. Amazing game.
10/10, you will spend hours on this game. Can be difficult, but always makes you come back time and time again.
This game is good at capturing the essence of the cold. Much like the cold embrace of my mother. Really fun strategy experience.

Thought this game was too easy since i breezed through the first 4 scenarios until the end of Winterhome lmao. Although the missions were optional, I don't think anyone is achieving those without knowing they're there from the start. Not to mention there are difficulty options, so definitely lots of challenge and replayability if you're a masochist and want to restart super long scenarios.
Super fun game. Simple enough to learn but complex enough to enjoy on a deeper level. Will have your head working so avoid playing right before bed.

God how do I start? This game is fantastic, I have very very little issues with the game.
I'll say the issue/s now: I wish I could increase the UI size, when I have a second display screen, this isn't a problem due to my second screen being much larger, but on my default laptop screen, the UI is very small and hard for me to read if I haven't played the game for a few days.
I do wish order and faith paths had a bit more differences between the two In most playthroughs now, I just end up picking order and only passing a few laws. This is more of a little pet peeve though.

[b]Anyway, onto the good parts of the game[/b]

After a rough start with many failures, I got the strat down to survive, the premise is fantastic, a world frozen over in the late 1800s? A steampunk like aesthetic? beautiful. The difficult start doesn't last for long once you get the gist of what you need to prioritise (It took me way too long to realise that I should be using gathering posts).
The small storylines and custom scenarios add a great flavour of lore without it taking too much of the gameplay, it leaves you wanting more, wanting to know what on earth happened to make it so cold, you feel like you're in the unknown like the rest of the people in this world.

The sounds of the UI are so well made to really add to the feel of cold, the subtle crackling of ice and the loud industrial sounds when you get notified of another lil automaton being spawned, it's beautiful.
The music overall? 10/10. ESPECIALLY during the storm, you feel the tension, the stress and you clench every known muscle in your body as you watch your city, unable to do anything but pray that they survive. This game really achieved that feeling of helplessness as a violently cold storm, unable to be stopped, slowly climbs your way.

I was gifted the second game and I am following the updates for 1886, I love this game, even though my playtime is a bit on the lower end for a game I love so much, I am captivated every minute I play it. 
I'm currently working on 100% this game before I fully start the second (I only played a tiny bit of the second and ngl, I am excited to try out the mods) 

This game is great in so many ways, definitely not a city builder that you can relax in, but it offers a very unique challenge and options for managing your city. You can be a benevolent manager, or a brutal dictator, it all depends of what laws you want to pass and how far you want to take those same laws. Overall, I recommend this game so much.
I could write so much more but then it would be an essay- So I shall stop the review here.

I could never resist a good strategy game with a story. When this first game was put on offer for the Frostpunk II release, I just had to try it out and it did not disappoint.
It has the familiar eerie style of other 11 Bit Studios (e.g. This War of Mine), great background music and plenty of bad vs. worse moral dilemmas. Simple mechanics allowed me to quickly get up to speed and managed full achievements on the first two stories on default difficulty. Difficulty can be finely tweaked to match your appetite for punishment.
A must-have for any casual strategy player.

After 76 hours in Frostpunk and unlocking every achievement, i can confidently say its one of the best strategy games Ive ever played.
At first, it looks like a typical city building game, but its so much more than that. Every decision matters, and youre constantly forced to choose between whats right and what will keep your city alive. The game does an amazing job of making you feel responsible for every single person under your leadership.
The atmosphere is incredible. The freezing temperatures, the soundtrack, and the constant pressure of survival keep you fully immersed from start to finish. Even after dozens of hours, some scenarios still managed to challenge me.
Getting all the achievements wasnt easy, but it pushed me to explore different strategies and experience everything the game has to offer.
Frostpunk is one of those rare games that stays with you long after youve finished playing. I highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys strategy, survival, and meaningful decision-making.

I've been needing a game like this. I am a father of 3, one being a tiny baby that wants to be held often. When putting him to sleep by holding and bouncing him, I only have 1 free hand so usually have to stop what I'm doing. But with this game, you can play entirely with just the mouse! Also you don't NEED sound at all. There's nothing gameplay critical in the sounds (although the sound is well designed) so I don't feel like I'm missing anything that prevents me from enjoying the game by having no sound on, and just using the mouse while I rock my baby to sleep. It's pretty addicting too! I just want to keep playing!
This game has always been a gem, a nice mix of challenge and builder sim.

Genuinely my favorite city builder/resource management game. You have to manage food and coal stores while keeping everyone warm so they don't get sick. You also have to gather steel and wood to build things. There's also laws you can enact like extended shifts and child labour. Later on you can choose to become a religious cult or a military dictatorship. The gameplay alone is stellar, but following the story cements it as one of the best strategy games out there. My current playtime doesn't include the roughly 30 hours I played on xbox.

Really fun survival city builder game. Unlike nothing I've ever played before. Highly recommended.

11/10

There's something really special about Frostpunk as a survival city builder. While the gameplay itself is limited (tech tree is relatively straightforward, laws are interesting but ultimately there's only so many possible decisions), it is extremely atmospheric and each scenario is difficult to put down.
Great story, nice graphics, quite challenging (on higher difficulties).
It's bleak. Three days without food, the corpses in the snow pit are looking tempting. My automaton just stepped on my last engineer and the cook lost his leg to frostbite. It's getting dark...

Jogaço da porra. 

Foi minha primeira experiência com um jogo city builder, e me fez ficar MUITO interessado pelo gênero. O jogo tem uma ambientação impecável, o frio, gerenciamento de recursos, os cidadãos fudidos sempre reclamando de tudo o que vc faz não importando o que seja.

Tomar uma decisão errada pode afetar o resto da sua run, levando a voltar um save ou começar do zero (o que eu acabei fazendo bastante), que também é a graça do jogo; recomeçar do zero é a melhor coisa que você pode fazer, aprendendo com o que vez de errado e tentar montar um assentamento melhor é uma das coisas mais satisfatórias de Frostpunk. Replayability 10/10

Although I've never been big on resource management, this ended up one of my favorite games back when it released. Started playing again 6 years later and it's still damn good, but hard as hell. That said, it doesn't run as smoothly as it did back then.

DO IT.... JUST DO IT. ONCE YOU PLAY YOU'll NEVER GO BACK. in reality this game will make you cry as you have to starve Little Timmy to death in order to get more coal but dame once you bet the game its satisifying
I really enjoyed this game and recomend it to anyone with the money  im not even into city builders and I really enjoyed it

It’s an excellent survival colony sim with a great setting and great story. Every decision matters, and the harsh environment forces players to carefully balance the needs of both the people and the city. The game offers great progression, challenging strategic choices, and some different scenarios and difficulty levels.
However, it lacks a proper endgame. As it is very scenario driven, but arfter you are done with a scenario, well you are done, you can't play continue to play in the city you just build, the scenario just ends. Even with teh endless dlc, now teh game is endless, but after you unlocked everything, it just gets boring.
But overall I recommend it, very great game.

I started this game maybe two or three times and quickly failed, then quit and didn't touch it again for awhile. Next playthrough it hooked me. Overall it's not really that steep of a learning curve and for me it started to get pretty satisfying after an hour or so. Played a few runs now, enjoyed it each time. Unique and memorable atmosphere, which I think is what separates it from similar games.
I would say its fire but it is cold at the peak. ABSOLUTLY PEAK MANAGEMENT SIM. Buy this if you love every management sim ever.
the most catchy game ive ever played it was 00:00 when i sat on the game and i played it for 5 hours straight and i know most people did that too

TFW you set out to save a city of innocents from an apocalyptic blizzard and accidentally crown yourself god king of mankind. Whoops.

All of us have played city survival games before. I fondly remember playing Rebuild on the school computers way back in the day among others. Frostpunk is very similar to those: Tense decision making, random events to throw a spanner into your plans, harrowing sacrifice, death defying gambles, and an amazing feeling of triumph when you barely manage to beat the odds. However, the game's progression and lack of proper guidance does bring it down a bit.

For one, the game does a horrible job of explaining its mechanics and what is actually expected of you. I did end up beating the game... on my 3rd attempt, because I genuinely did not understand a lot of the game's systems as they're explained terribly. I frequently ran out of resources not knowing something had happened to my workers or buildings, the UI does a bad job of highlighting the most important information on any given building, and there are so many buttons and icons absolutely everywhere that it's nearly impossible to keep track of. It would have been so much better if things like building abilities were all consolidated in one place, so you wouldn't have to constantly click on and search for your buildings in order to use them or check their cooldowns. Then multiple hours in: [spoiler] You suddenly have an extremely tight deadline dropped on you out of nowhere. If you're struggling at this point in the game, you might as well just reset the entire run and try again, because at that point you'll just spend the next hour watching your empire collapse before your eyes. There is no recovering. At no point does the game ever foreshadow this, and it wouldn't have been a problem if it was and the game gave you at least a little bit more time to prepare, but alas. The storm is a great emotional climax where you get to see the fruits of your labour play out before your eyes as some incredible music plays all throughout, but it feels so cheap on the first playthrough, which takes away from it quite a bit. [/spoiler]

Also, you are HEAVILY punished for not knowing exactly what's coming and how best to deal with it. The game gives you absolutely no time whatsoever to get your bearings and learn what's going on. If you aren't planning 3 steps ahead and firing on all cylinders at all times from the word go, you will not beat the game. Full stop. Every single person in your city needs to be working around the clock, you need constant, massive influxes of all 4 resources, you need to be researching and spamming your abilities on cooldown, and you need to plan your city in advance even when you don't know what buildings and abilities you'll have in the future. If you don't do any of this, then you'll spend around 6 hours straight in a perpetual death spiral, constantly out of resources with your citizens dropping like flies. At that point, you might as well just start a new run, because again, there is no recovering lost time. [spoiler] and if you send your scouts to the wrong location early in the run, the Londoners will form almost immediately, meaning more spinning plates to manage from earlier in the game. [/spoiler]

Streets are also just a super annoying mechanic. All buildings need to have streets connected to the generator in the middle of the city to function, effectively meaning that all buildings cost a couple extra units of wood and a few extra seconds of construction time every time you build one. It's completely pointless as a mechanic, and all it does is just waste your time and potentially gimp your production if you misclick and disconnect a building from the generator by a single tile. Building far away from the center is already punished by the cold; just have the streets be automatic and increase the cost of buildings if it's for balancing.

The game seems determined to really judge the player's actions with a kind of pseudo morality system, but it falls completely flat. There are really only like 2 or 3 laws you can pass that are cartoonishly evil, with all the rest being completely understandable in a post-apocalyptic society. [spoiler] At the end of the game, the game seemed like it was gearing up to really let loose on every terrible thing I'd apparently done, only for the only things it was able to yell at me for were having children serve soup and having murderers and thieves publically humiliated, before dramatically asking "Did we go too far?". No! No we did not. We just survived the apocalypse with fewer than 50 people dead out of a city of more than 300. Little timmy gathering firewood and some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ getting beat up in public for stealing food is chump change. Please stop trying to make games into Spec Ops: the Line. That game and its emotionally manipulative BS are bad. [/spoiler]

Complaints aside, Frostpunk is a really tense, emotional and satisfying city sim. It's just unfortunate that so much of the game's enjoyment relies on a full understanding of all of its systems and upgrades before you even press start.

One of the most challenging city building games i've played but also still enjoyable to replay since the covid19 pandemic. Love the voice acting and the immersive stories as the great frost nears. After buying the DLC's during a massive sale, my pc can't handle playing them. Thankfully i can still play with the Base Game <3

"10/10 for both games. Love them so much I'd reset a hundred times and accept it gladly."

I play Frostpunk on another platform with all the DLC already, not on Steam. But let me tell you—no matter where you play it, the experience is just as intense.

What makes me love this game and give it a 10/10 without a second thought isn't just the gameplay. It's the setting of the world.

A world so freezing cold it chills you to the bone. Civilization has collapsed. The last remnants of humanity huddle together around a central generator in the middle of the city, struggling to survive blizzards reaching negative 150 degrees. That feeling of utter despair, yet with a faint glimmer of hope flickering in the endless cold... it's so incredibly powerful. I got completely immersed. The moment I clicked start, I sank deep into it and never looked back.

Another thing I love, without needing to waste any time on headaches, is the sheer polish of the game's systems. Seriously. This game doesn't require you to mess around with endless settings. You don't need to download Mods to fix the balance. Everything is designed perfectly straight out of the box. The interface is clear. The information is dense but never confusing. The concentric rings around the generator, letting you slowly expand your city at your own pace... it's both beautiful and logical. 11 bit studios really respects its players. Honestly, you just open the game and play. No need to configure a single extra thing. I absolutely love that.

But... ugh... now we arrive at the most painful part.

As a Perfectionist who craves extreme challenge and demands that everything is precise down to the last detail...

This very nature is what makes me hit Reset tens, even hundreds of times.

I don't just want to survive. I want the city to run like clockwork. Every steam hub placed at the optimal distance. Every worker assigned to the right job at the right hour. Every research queued in the perfect order. Every law signed at the exact moment it yields maximum benefit with minimum consequence.

Because Frostpunk is a game that punishes mistakes silently. Little by little. Bit by bit. Until it all explodes in your face later on. You make a tiny mistake on Day 3. You placed a steam hub two hours too late. Three extra workers fell ill. Or you were too kind and accepted a group of refugees a little too early. Those tiny, insignificant errors slowly accumulate, like a domino effect. And then, by Day 20, hope is plummeting, discontent is skyrocketing, and the entire city is rioting. The scene before you is absolute, catastrophic failure.

And for someone who hates imperfection and craves precision like me... seeing those red graphs, seeing patients overflowing the medical posts because my plan started to leak... it's the most irritating, unbearable sight in the world.

I refuse to grit my teeth and scrape by, limping along day by day.

Instead, I will reset and start over immediately. I'll plan more precisely than before. I'll rearrange the entire build timeline from scratch. I'll make it seamless. Every single variable accounted for. Every possible outcome calculated. I'll make it as perfect as a single human brain can possibly manage.

Because the difficulty I seek isn't just about winning.

It's about winning flawlessly.

And when that day finally arrives... the day we survive the Great Storm. The day we push through temperatures of -150 degrees. The day our city functions with absolute precision. No pointless deaths. No riots. No wasted resources. No leaks in the system. Every single function I laid out responds exactly as planned...

That feeling is pure ecstasy.

10/10 for both games. It's a game built for people who love a challenge, who crave punishing difficulty, who demand tight and precise systems, and who are willing to drown themselves in an endless cycle of resets... all in the ruthless pursuit of perfection.

People like me.
great music, immersive world

A good game, but lacks the ability to automate and make pipelines for production. Very pretty though, and with an amazing soundtrack! I'd say you could get about 15 hours out of the base game. 
Pretty good, but wait to buy until its on sale :)
Great dystopian game, can you keep the moral up while the frost gets worse? How long can you survive without relying on faith or violent control?
A very good mix of city builder and survival strategy game, and the atmosphere is perfect. Very recommended if you're into those sort of things. Takes a bit of time to get better at.

There are some aspects that you can cheese like swapping workers around to work during the day then go hunt during the night so they don't rest. Prisoners don't seem to heal themselves if sick and a couple other situations which can be a bit odd or mechanics that aren't exactly shown well in the game like scouts taking less time going to already explored areas. (game doesn't prompt you to say this is the case and you wouldn't think to try going to an already depleted node)

Despite these small issues and "quirks" the game has this continuous tension between the overseer/captain, aka the player, and the struggling city. Various difficulties and modes would make it enjoyable for all. Highly recommend it over the sequel.
Frostpunk is one of the best city builders I have ever played. I dont think there is something like it.

awesome fantastic but bit critical lethargic game. one little mistake will make you frausted but when you skill the mechanics psychology u will be enjoy lots. this game will make a psycho emotional connection with you and after complete any scanario you will start another one asap like a series of good movies
fun game to pass the time with. love the theme, the looks, the gameplay of this game. Highly Recommend this fellas.

A very powerful experience, and shines at leaving you at odds at not knowing if you will be okay.

It asks you to think of the morality and morale of your people, while leaving the player to face the ethics of their decisions. It's aesthetically pleasing, and has a beautiful, memorable soundtrack.

One playthrough of the official main story is all you need to fully experience what this game has to offer.

It massively improves on their previous title, This War Of Mine, in a powerful way.

fire sound track, fire lore, fire artwork, fire gameplay mechanics (never even played a city builder before and I enjoyed it), amazing game, 10/10 no notes whatsoever. I haven't seen any devs move like this since no man's sky started getting good through updates.

I personally hate it, but you should buy to try it for because it's objectively a great game. worst case scenario, you refund

have a good day
-Fellon11

I really wish Steam has a neutral button, because there are some gaming experience where you didn't enjoy the game that much, but you didn't regret playing it either, and that is the case for me here. Had I bought this game at full price, I would probably be hoping for a refund. Thankfully, I got it at 90% discount, so it's good. Haha.

I don't know, the game wasn't doing it for me. It wasn't as fun and engaging as I hoped for. The reviews are great and the premise of the game is interesting so I was always curious about this game. But nah, it's not for me unfortunately.

Hard game for people that like being stepped on , does not feel grindy, atmosphere is awesome and sound effects are really nice.

I played 5 hours and they were enough to decide it was not for me.
I cant invest myself in a game for 5+ hours just to understand the basics just to handle a barrage of RNG events.
Very stressing.
Some people will love it.

Also, damn you londoners, I hope you freeze to death.

Strategy for people, who don't play strategies. Basic mechanics filled with micromanagment and lack of automatization or proper information displayed suggests pure button smashing between buldings to survive, because game constantly throws in your way hundreds of stupid micro-events. Looks fantastic, but sounds boring and doesn't owe you with anything that you didn't seen already. I was very upset when this game turned out to be simple bar fill management and not at least honest city builder.
I had a game breaking bug where my temperature went to like -1000000 degrees in my IRON Man save and killed my playthrough.

I was actually having fun with this game but it KEEPS CRASHING. I really want to play it but I just can't progress. It crashes 50% of the time on open, then it crashes when I loa the game, and if I get through all that, it crashes randomly when playing.

I'm playing on M1 Mac (I know, but it's all I have) so this review may only apply to other mac players.

Otherwise I was having fun when it was actually working.
Crashes on Mac M4 are constant... not recommended because of that. But the game is actually fun and challenging

For a 9  Euro purchase it is alright. You will spend a few hour to learn the mechanics of the game.
Otherwise not worthed
Damn I wanted to play like half an hour and ended up finishing a 5-hour run. Felt amazing btw, gonna repeat. 10/10

«Это единственная игра, где я поднял налог на кашу, запретил людям хоронить умерших, заставил детей копать уголь, а в конце всё равно получил сообщение: "Мы прошли через ад, но сохранили человечность". Какое, нахрен, человечность? Я тут генерал-инквизитор хлебопёк!

Первые 10 минут: "Ой, бедные люди, их дом сдуло, давайте построим тепло." Через час: "Если этот мужик ещё раз попросит еду вместо опилок — отправлю его собирать паровые ядра голыми руками!"

Особенно прикалывают законы. Третий уровень «Новой веры» — и вот у меня уже есть священный отряд мордоворотов, которые заставляют бабок молиться на теплотрассу. А закон о «Детских вышках»? Я думал, там горки и качели. НЕТ, ТАМ СМЕНА 2/2 ПО ЗАГРУЗКЕ УГЛЯ. ЗАБЕРИТЕ МОЮ ЛИЦЕНЗИЮ МАМЫ, Я ЧУДОВИЩЕ.

Финальный шторм. Температура -150°C. Мой генератор работает на пределе, люди едят суп из крыс и старых перчаток, а один умник пишет в чате: "Сэр, ваш декрет требует работать по 24 часа, но мы устали". РЕБЯТА, ВАМ ПОВЕЗЛО, ЧТО ВЫ ЕЩЁ НЕ В КОТЛЕ.

В общем, после этой игры я пошёл обнимать свою батарею и сказал ей, что она самая горячая. Фростпанк — это симулятор капитана "Титаника", где ты сам пилишь айсберг на дрова. P.S. Собаку свою в игре я назвал "Уголёк". Не кормил три дня. Она выжила и помогла разморозить шахты. Топ дагестанер игры.»

The first thing I do in Frostpunk is research "Little Hands" that allows me to [strike] exploit children [/strike] build children's character by having my tiny unionized penguins march through the −40°C weather to collect coal and wood. That alone makes Frostpunk perfect in my book.

[h2] The City Must Survive, and My VRAM is Leading the Charge [/h2]

After nearly 100 hours of staring into the frozen abyss, I’ve realized that Frostpunk isn't just a city-builder, it’s a high-stakes Resource Management & Maintenance Simulator. As an aircraft technician, keeping the Generator humming at -150°C feels exactly like a critical engine troubleshoot, zero room for error.
[olist]
    [*]Visuals & Performance
Running this on the RX 9060 XT is a treat. The heat distortion from the steam vents and the way the frost creeps across the screen is breathtaking. It’s the only game that makes me feel cold even when my PC is pumping out enough heat to warm my room.
    [*]The "Iron Man" Mentality
Getting the "Iron Man" achievement (The Arks) is my proudest moment. It requires the kind of precision where a single minute of downtime or a mismanaged coal pile leads to total system failure. No active pause in Survivor mode? That's just real-time troubleshooting under pressure.
    [*]Ethics vs. Efficiency 
Most games ask if you want to be "Good" or "Evil." Frostpunk asks if you want to be "Efficient" or "Dead." I’ve signed laws that would make a dictator blush, but hey at least the Generator is still humming and the soup is (mostly) warm.
    [*]The Verdict
If you enjoy optimizing heat maps, calculating coal-to-steam ratios, and losing sleep over a fictional city’s survival, this is the gold standard. It’s brutal, beautiful, and highly addictive.
[/olist]
